description:
You are a data-management assistant helping a life-science researcher organise two small experimental datasets that already live on the workstation.  
Follow the steps below strictly inside the terminal.  When you finish, the system must contain the exact files, database objects and reports described here so that an automated grader can verify your work.

Existing data (already present before you start)
1. /home/user/datasets/experimentsA.csv  
2. /home/user/datasets/experimentsB.csv  

Both files are UTF-8 encoded, comma-separated and have a single header row.  Do **not** modify them.  You will see their contents when you open them in the terminal.

Your tasks
1. Create the directory /home/user/research if it does not already exist.  
2. Inside that directory create an SQLite database named experiment_results.sqlite.
3. Inside the database create two tables with the exact names experiment_a and experiment_b whose schema matches the two CSVs (three TEXT columns called sample_id, condition, value).  
4. Import the rows from experimentsA.csv into experiment_a and from experimentsB.csv into experiment_b.  
5. Create a database VIEW named combined_results that exactly …  
   • Selects all rows from experiment_a and experiment_b,  
   • Adds a 4th column called dataset that has the literal text 'A' for rows coming from experiment_a and 'B' for rows coming from experiment_b.  
   • The column order in the view must be: sample_id, condition, value, dataset.
6. Inside the same database create a TABLE named condition_summary whose contents are obtained from combined_results by  
   • grouping by condition  
   • computing the average of value (cast to REAL) per condition,  
   • rounding that average to three decimal places,  
   • and storing it in a column named avg_value (REAL).  
   The column order must be: condition, avg_value.
7. Export the full contents of condition_summary to /home/user/research/summary_report.csv.  
   The CSV must  
   • be UTF-8,  
   • contain a single header row exactly: condition,avg_value  
   • list the rows ordered alphabetically by condition,  
   • show avg_value rounded to three decimal places (e.g. 0.536).  The automated test will compare the file byte-for-byte.
8. Create a plain-text log file /home/user/research/import_log.txt with the following exact layout (four lines, no extra spaces):  
   experiment_a: <ROWCOUNT_A>  
   experiment_b: <ROWCOUNT_B>  
   view_combined_results: OK  
   summary_export: OK  
   where <ROWCOUNT_A> and <ROWCOUNT_B> are the integer numbers of rows you actually imported from the respective CSVs.  The grader will check the counts against the files on disk.

Important output specifications that WILL be automatically checked
• /home/user/research/experiment_results.sqlite must exist.  
• Tables experiment_a, experiment_b and condition_summary as well as the view combined_results must exist in that database with the exact names and column order given.  
• /home/user/research/summary_report.csv must exactly match the format described in step 7.  
• /home/user/research/import_log.txt must contain exactly four lines following the template shown in step 8.  

Work only with regular user privileges; do not use sudo.  When everything is finished, leave all created artefacts in place for grading.
truth:
Pre-existing files (created during task initialisation):
1. /home/user/datasets/experimentsA.csv  
   Exact content (6 lines):
   sample_id,condition,value
   S1,control,0.50
   S2,treatment,1.20
   S3,treatment,1.10
   S4,control,0.45
   S5,treatment,1.30

2. /home/user/datasets/experimentsB.csv  
   Exact content (6 lines):
   sample_id,condition,value
   B1,control,0.55
   B2,control,0.60
   B3,treatment,1.25
   B4,treatment,1.15
   B5,control,0.58


Files/directories that must exist when the agent completes the task:

1. Directory
   /home/user/research
   (permissions 755, owned by user)

2. SQLite database
   /home/user/research/experiment_results.sqlite  
   Containing:
   • Table experiment_a (columns: sample_id TEXT, condition TEXT, value TEXT) with exactly 5 rows whose content equals experimentsA.csv lines 2-6.  
   • Table experiment_b (same schema) with exactly 5 rows whose content equals experimentsB.csv lines 2-6.  
   • View combined_results created by  
       SELECT sample_id, condition, value, 'A' AS dataset FROM experiment_a  
       UNION ALL  
       SELECT sample_id, condition, value, 'B' AS dataset FROM experiment_b;  
     The view must expose four columns in that order.  
   • Table condition_summary (columns: condition TEXT, avg_value REAL) with exactly 2 rows:  
       Row 1 → control | 0.536  
       Row 2 → treatment | 1.200

3. CSV export
   /home/user/research/summary_report.csv  
   Exact byte-for-byte content (including final newline):
   condition,avg_value
   control,0.536
   treatment,1.200

4. Log file
   /home/user/research/import_log.txt  
   Exact content (text, four lines, final newline at end):
   experiment_a: 5
   experiment_b: 5
   view_combined_results: OK
   summary_export: OK
